Article Title: DMK publication hits out at Rahul for his 'lack of gratitude'
Article Text:
Rahul Gandhi





CHENNAI: DMK mouthpiece Murasoli on Saturday attacked Congress and criticised leader of the opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi 's "lack of gratitude" after his party moved out of the DMK-led alliance and teamed up with TVK in Tamil Nadu .In its editorial, Murasoli recalled the relationship between DMK and Congress since 2018, when former CM M K Stalin announced Rahul as the prime ministerial candidate for the first time. "Does Rahul have any gratitude for the gesture?" DMK asked."The seeds for the INDIA bloc were sown in Tamil Nadu. It was Stalin who initiated efforts to build a national opposition alliance against BJP ahead of the 2024 Lok Sabha elections. It was due to the INDIA bloc that BJP couldn't reach 370 or 400 seats," DMK said. "Even when former CMs such as K Chandrashekar Rao and Mamata Banerjee didn't want Congress in the INDIA bloc, Stalin stayed strong that Congress should be part of the INDIA bloc," said the editorial.Congress members, including Praveen Chakravarty, who were close to Rahul, repeatedly targeted the DMK govt and its "Dravidian model" of governance, the editorial alleged. "The DMK govt was not allowed to function peacefully, and all this happened with Rahul's knowledge," it said.
Article Summary: DMK mouthpiece Murasoli attacked Congress and criticised leader of the opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi's "lack of gratitude" after his party moved out of the DMK-led alliance and teamed up with TVK in Tamil Nadu. "Does Rahul have any gratitude for the gesture?" DMK asked.

Article Title: Decades after Syama Prasad's death, a day of pride for family
Article Text:
West Bengal BJP chief Samik Bhattacharya pays tribute to Syama Prasad Mookerjee in Bhawanipur





KOLKATA: Pride and happiness defined the day for the kin of Syama Prasad Mookerjee as PM Narendra Modi and all other senior BJP leaders recalled his sacrifices and paid rich tributes to the founder of Bharatiya Jan Sangh. After the swearing-in ceremony, newly elected CM Suvendu Adhikari visited Mukherjee's home and paid tribute to the party icon. For Mookerjee's granddaughter Sarmeela Marquis, it was a great day as the name of her "Dadu" echoed through the voices of all politicians on Saturday. "To keep his memory alive, the newly elected govt must work to turn his dreams into reality," she said. Justice Chittatosh Mookerjee, former chief justice of Bombay high court and nephew of Syama Prasad, said: "It's good to see Mejka's (my uncle's) photograph on the front page of newspapers decades after his death in a Srinagar jail in 1953. His mother Jogmaya Devi had appealed to the then PM Jawaharlal Nehru for an independent inquiry, but it was not accepted."
Article Summary: Pride and happiness defined the day for the kin of Syama Prasad Mookerjee. PM Narendra Modi and all other senior BJP leaders recalled his sacrifices. West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari visited Mukherjee's home and paid tribute.

Article Title: What if Vijay had joined in 2009, Congress wonders
Article Text: NEW DELHI: As TVK chief Vijay moves to govern Tamil Nadu after upending the state's politics in the assembly polls, questions are being asked if Congress' fortunes in the state would have taken a giant leap had the superstar's interaction with Rahul Gandhi during UPA's tenure materialised into something concrete.The questions have acquired salience after the surfacing of an old photograph of Vijay with then NSUI office-bearers - now MP Hibi Eden, AICC spokesperson Ragini Nayak, AICC secretary Gopinath Palaniyappan and Ashok Basoya. Vijay had then met Rahul, who, fresh from Congress' victory in the 2009 Lok Sabha elections, was aiming at a generational makeover of the party.By his own admission in a viral video, Vijay had taken a day off from his shoot schedule to meet Rahul with his father. He was thrilled by the interaction but denied any move to join Congress.Some in Congress say Vijay was asked by Rahul to go through the drill of contesting Youth Congress elections, which the former was reluctant to do. The lingering regret in Congress ranks seems to be that if Vijay had joined the party, Congress could have been at the core of Tamil Nadu politics today.If not then, now. The partnership is happening 17 years later.Since their first meeting, Rahul and Vijay have been in touch - the reason there was such a push to ally with TVK in the run-up to state polls, which was nixed by Congress leadership and the state unit.But after the polls threw up a hung assembly and DMK was left without the possibility of forming govt, Congress decided to join hands with TVK. Congress' outreach with CPM, CPI and VCK is said to have played a role in the three parties following suit.When Rahul attends Vijay's oath-taking ceremony in Chennai on Sunday, it would mark the culmination of what was initiated years ago.
Article Summary: TVK chief Vijay is set to be sworn in as Tamil Nadu chief minister on Sunday. Questions are being asked if Congress' fortunes in the state would have taken a giant leap had the superstar's interaction with Rahul Gandhi during UPA's tenure materialised into something concrete.

Article Title: Tvk Government Formation: Evening news wrap: VCK, IUML back Vijay’s TVK government formation; India successfully tests Advanced Agni missile with MIRV capability and more
Article Text:
Evening news wrap

VCK and IUML back Vijay’s TVK, helping the party cross majority mark to form Tamil Nadu government.

India successfully tests Advanced Agni missile with MIRV capability.

PM Modi bows to Bengal as Suvendu Adhikari takes oath as the state’s first BJP chief minister.

Mamata Banerjee updates X bio after TMC defeat.

ED arrests Punjab minister Sanjeev Arora in alleged money laundering and fake GST billing case.

Tamil Nadu: TVK crosses majority mark as VCK, IUML extend support

Watch VCK Backs Vijay As TVK Moves Closer To Forming Government In Tamil Nadu | Watch VCK Backs Vijay As TVK Moves Closer To Forming Government In Tamil Nadu | Watch

India successfully tests Advanced Agni missile with MIRV capability

PM Modi bows to Bengal as Suvendu Adhikari takes oath as first BJP CM

Mamata changes X bio after BJP win, signals end of TMC rule in Bengal

ED arrests Punjab minister Sanjeev Arora in money laundering case





Here are the top stories of the day:Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) crossed the majority mark in the Tamil Nadu Assembly after VCK and IUML extended support to Vijay-led party’s bid to form the government. VCK chief Thol Thirumavalavan formally handed over a support letter, taking TVK’s tally to 119, before IUML support pushed it to 120 MLAs. Meanwhile, DMK chief MK Stalin congratulated the incoming government. Follow live updates India announced the successful flight-test of an Advanced Agni missile equipped with Multiple Independently Targeted Re-Entry Vehicle (MIRV) technology, significantly boosting the country’s strategic strike capability. Conducted from Dr APJ Abdul Kalam Island off the Odisha coast, the missile carried multiple payloads aimed at separate targets across the Indian Ocean Region. Read full story Prime Minister Narendra Modi bowed before the people of West Bengal with folded hands during the swearing-in ceremony of Suvendu Adhikari as the state’s first BJP chief minister. Held at Kolkata’s Brigade Parade Ground, the event marked the end of the Trinamool Congress’ 15-year rule after BJP won 207 seats in the 2026 Assembly polls.Days after refusing to resign following the Trinamool Congress’ defeat in the West Bengal Assembly elections, Mamata Banerjee appears to have acknowledged the end of her government by updating her X bio to describe herself as former chief minister of the 15th, 16th and 17th Assemblies. The move comes after BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari took oath as Bengal’s first BJP chief minister. Read full story The Enforcement Directorate arrested Punjab industries minister and AAP leader Sanjeev Arora in a money laundering case after conducting raids across Delhi, Gurugram and Chandigarh. The agency alleged Arora and linked entities used fake mobile phone purchase bills through shell firms to claim fraudulent GST benefits and later routed funds via Dubai. Read full story
Article Summary: India successfully tests Advanced Agni missile with MIRV capability. PM Modi bows to Bengal as Suvendu Adhikari takes oath as the state’s first BJP chief minister. Punjab minister Sanjeev Arora arrested in alleged money laundering and fake GST billing case.

Article Title: Has Mamata Banerjee accepted defeat? What her new X bio says
Article Text:
Watch 'I Will Not Resign', Mamata Alleges EC Bias, Says TMC Won ‘Morally’ After Bengal Election Loss 'I Will Not Resign', Mamata Alleges EC Bias, Says TMC Won ‘Morally’ After Bengal Election Loss

Poll Do you believe the recent West Bengal elections were conducted fairly? Yes, absolutely fair. No, there was malpractice.





NEW DELHI: Just days after refusing to step down following the Trinamool Congress ’ crushing defeat in the West Bengal Assembly elections, Mamata Banerjee appears to have quietly acknowledged the end of her government.The West Bengal chief minister’s bio on X now describes her as “Founder Chairperson All India Trinamool Congress. Chief Minister of West Bengal (15th, 16th and 17th Vidhan Sabha).” The earlier version of bio read: “Founder Chairperson All India Trinamool Congress. Honourable Chief Minister, West Bengal.”This comes less than a week after Mamata had insisted she would not resign despite the BJP’s sweeping victory in the state.“I won’t resign. Let them dismiss me,” Banerjee said while urging party workers to observe a “black day” during the first sitting of the new Assembly. She also asked TMC legislators to wear black clothes inside the House as a mark of protest.Also read| From Dilip Ghosh to Agnimitra Paul - what BJP’s first West Bengal cabinet looks like Banerjee had alleged large-scale electoral malpractice and accused the BJP, Election Commission, CRPF and sections of the police administration of targeting her party during the polls.“This was not an election but an atrocity,” she had said, while also claiming that more than 1,500 TMC offices had been “hijacked”.The TMC later said the Kalighat meeting was aimed at preparing the party for its role in the opposition benches and reaffirmed that it would continue to fight “BJP’s tyranny and oppression tooth and nail”.On Saturday, Suvendu Adhikari was sworn in as the state’s first BJP chief minister since Independence. Governor RN Ravi administered the oath at a grand ceremony in Kolkata attended by Prime Minister Narendra Modi and senior BJP leaders.Also read| 'I bow to West Bengal Jana Shakti': PM Modi kowtows before Kolkata crowd as BJP forms first govt Adhikari’s rise marks a symbolic reversal in Bengal politics. Once one of Banerjee’s closest lieutenants and a key architect of the 2007 Nandigram movement that helped end Left Front rule, he later defected to the BJP in 2020 amid growing tensions with the TMC leadership. In the 2026 Assembly elections, he defeated the TMC in both Nandigram and Bhabanipur, while the BJP secured 207 seats to end the party’s 15-year rule in the state.
Article Summary: Mamata Banerjee appears to have quietly acknowledged the end of her government. The West Bengal chief minister’s bio on X now describes her as “Founder Chairperson All India Trinamool Congress. Chief Minister of West Bengal (15th, 16th and 17th Vidhan Sabha)” This comes less than a week after Mamata had insisted she would not resign.

Article Title: Ex-Chief Justice TS Sivagnanam resigns from SIR tribunal, says it will take four years to clear Kolkata appeals
Article Text:
Former Chief Justice of Calcutta High Court, Justice TS Sivagnanam

Watch From Corruption To Citizenship: How TMC & BJP Fought The SIR-Changed Bengal Polls From Corruption To Citizenship: How TMC & BJP Fought The SIR-Changed Bengal Polls

KOLKATA: Former Chief Justice of Calcutta High Court, Justice TS Sivagnanam, on Thursday resigned from the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) tribunal on personal grounds. After clearing 1,777 appeals over 22 days and rejecting none, Justice Sivagnanam said at its present pace, the tribunal would need four years to clear all Kolkata appeals before it.He submitted his resignation to Calcutta HC Chief Justice Sujoy Paul, as well as to the Election Commission Initially, Justice Sivagnanam was appointed to the tribunal for petitioners from North 24 Parganas and Kolkata but he also conducted hearings for cases from Malda, Murshidabad and Birbhum as they were referred to him by the Supreme Court.Md Mahatab Sheikh, a Congress candidate from Farakka, was the first to have his name cleared by the tribunal headed by Justice Sivagnanam. Sheikh's voting rights were restored and he won the seat to become an MLA.Justice Sivagnanam, who will be flying back to Chennai on Friday, said: "I cleared 1,777 appeals between April 5 and 27. I even heard the appeals of Suprabuddha Sen, the grandson of eminent painter Nandalal Bose, and his wife Deepa Sen and restored their voting rights. I approached the appeals with an open mind. Later, I verified the appeals from Kolkata South and Kolkata North.I operated daily from 8.30am to 5pm to clear as many cases as I could, and even on Sundays, I cleared cases without any staff. ""I heard the appeals of the nuns of Missionaries of Charity and allowed 30 or more of them to vote. Although the certificates of the nuns did not match those from their pre-order lives, I did not reject any appeal," he added.Justice Sivagnanam felt that it would take another four years to completely process the hearings for Kolkata. "There are still 1 lakh pending appeals. There were some problems with the online appeals; judges are accustomed to giving long orders but the portal has word-count restrictions, so I started giving orders in four to five lines to accommodate this. Actually, I am accustomed to e-court proceedings, but not all ex-judges are tech-savvy. Although I am not a technocrat, I understand the login process and how to upload orders — as I have experienced it before — but other retired judges might not have the same experience. The technical support from the state govt for the 19 tribunals was excellent," he said.The former judge noted that there is a need for a special button in the system so that a notice can be sent to applicants who have appealed.
Article Summary: Former Chief Justice of Calcutta High Court, Justice TS Sivagnanam, on Thursday resigned from the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) tribunal on personal grounds. After clearing 1,777 appeals over 22 days and rejecting none, he said at its present pace, the tribunal would need four years to clear all Kolkata appeals.

Article Title: Kolkata Police unfollows Mamata Banerjee, Abhishek Banerjee on X; follows PM Narendra Modi, Amit Shah after BJP win
Article Text:
Watch Governor RN Ravi Dissolves West Bengal Assembly After Mamata Banerjee Refuses To Resign Governor RN Ravi Dissolves West Bengal Assembly After Mamata Banerjee Refuses To Resign

Poll How do you feel about the Kolkata Police unfollowing Mamata Banerjee and her associates? Supportive Indifferent Opposed

KOLKATA: Following the BJP's landmark victory in the Bengal assembly elections, the Kolkata Police's official X (formerly Twitter) handle has undergone a symbolic digital shift.The account has reportedly now unfollowed former chief minister Mamata Banerjee , Trinamool national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee, among a few others.In a move signalling alignment with the changing political landscape, the handle now follows PM Narendra Modi, home minister Amit Shah and the official accounts of the PMO and MHA.This transition coincides with the withdrawal of additional security from Trinamool Congress headquarters and netas' homes across the city.
Article Summary: The Kolkata Police's official X (formerly Twitter) handle has undergone a symbolic digital shift. The account has reportedly now unfollowed former chief minister Mamata Banerjee. The handle now follows PM Narendra Modi, home minister Amit Shah and the official accounts of the PMO and MHA.
